text.title: GraphQL API Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules every GraphQL API application must enforce. These are HARD rules — implementation expedience does not override them.

rule.statement: Schema is authored in SDL (.graphql files); resolvers are generated or hand-written against the SDL. No 'resolver-first' that emits SDL later.
rule.rationale: Resolver-first drift between resolver shape and schema makes the schema unreliable; SDL-first is the only single source.

rule.statement: Server and client types are generated from SDL (graphql-codegen, gqlgen, Pothos). Manual type declarations duplicating SDL are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Hand-typed shapes drift; codegen guarantees alignment.

rule.statement: Every 1:N relation traversed in a resolver uses a per-request DataLoader. Plain repo.findByXxx calls inside list resolvers are blocked by lint.
rule.rationale: Without DataLoader, a list of 100 items with a relation fires 101 queries; with DataLoader, 2 queries.

rule.statement: Schema runs with depth limit (5-8 default) and query complexity cap (configurable). Plugin runs before resolvers; rejects queries that exceed.
rule.rationale: Without caps, hostile clients craft DoS queries (deeply nested introspection); caps are the only practical defence.

rule.statement: Lists use Relay-style cursor pagination: edges + node + pageInfo with hasNextPage + endCursor. Offset/limit lists are forbidden for paginated resources.
rule.rationale: Offset pagination breaks under concurrent inserts; cursor is consistent under writes.

rule.statement: Every PR touching schema.graphql runs graphql-inspector (or equivalent) against main. Breaking changes block merge without explicit BREAKING-CHANGE token in commit body.
rule.rationale: Breaking changes shipped silently break clients; CI gate makes the decision visible.

rule.statement: When preconditions in Applies-If hold and no Skip-If trigger applies, walk the procedure end-to-end and run the validator before declaring done.

rationale.source: F-066 v3 B5.6 — mandatory run leaf

rule.rationale: Decision-tree leaves must reference a rule id. The run leaf maps "preconditions met" to the canonical execution path.

rule.statement: Skip this methodology when the Applies-If preconditions are not met OR any Skip-If disqualifier holds.

rationale.source: F-066 v3 B5.6 — mandatory skip leaf

rule.rationale: Decision-tree leaves must reference a rule id. The skip leaf needs a real rule so non-applicable inputs route cleanly out instead of producing false-positive verdicts.
